candidate_product_concepts.name: Consumer Discovery MVP (Phase 1 Focus)
candidate_product_concepts.description: Implementation focused exclusively on Pillar 1 (Home Real Property Assistant) to prioritize user acquisition, property data ingestion (images, 3D splats), and service provider matching for individual homeowners.
who_it_serves: Property investors managing one or more residences
candidate_product_concepts.problem_it_solves: Property owners wasting time finding reliable contractors, comparing inconsistent bids, and managing follow-up/scheduling.

assumptions.inference: The MVP should prioritize the 'AI Agent' discovery experience over a manual directory.
assumptions.basis: Raw Intent Vision Statement: 'minimize level of effort for both parties' via 'AI Agent or Hestami Staff facilitate service provider discovery'.

assumptions.inference: Vendor onboarding is treated as an external process (non-internal platform requirement) for V1.
assumptions.basis: Raw Intent Pillar 1 Domain 9: 'External Vendor Coordination - Service provider tracking without onboarding requirements'.

assumptions.inference: Offline capabilities for homeowners are secondary to the primary web-based experience.
assumptions.basis: Raw Intent Architecture: 'Mobile... Dedicated clients for homeowners and technicians, focusing on task-centric workflows and offline-first capabilities' (Implies offline is critical for Technicians, not necessarily Homeowners).

assumptions.inference: The 'Digital Asset' ingestion (3D Gaussian Splats, images) is a prerequisite for the service matching logic.
assumptions.basis: Raw Intent Mission Statement: 'leveraging modern techniques such 3D Gaussian Splats... to minimize friction'.

assumptions.inference: Accounting/Financials (Pillar 2/3) are strictly out of scope for this MVP.
assumptions.basis: Raw Intent Phasing Strategy: Phase 1 is 'Hestami Home Real Property Assistant' distinct from Phase 2/3.

constraints: Must use SvelteKit for the frontend portal.
Must use Bun for high-performance API execution.
Must use DBOS versioned workflows for the service call lifecycle.
Must enforce Row-Level Security (RLS) for multi-tenant isolation.
Must use SeaweedFS for media asset storage.
Must strictly follow the defined pillars (Phase 1 only) for feature gating.
open_questions: How to monetize the consumer-facing MVP without violating privacy or trust models?
What is the specific retention strategy for homeowners who only use the app for a single service call?
Does the MVP include the Human Hestami Staff service call execution, or is it fully AI/Agent driven?

candidate_product_concepts.name: Enterprise Governance MVP (Phase 3 Focus)
candidate_product_concepts.description: Implementation focused exclusively on Pillar 3 (Community Association Management) to prioritize compliance, financial governance, and vendor management for HOAs and management companies.
candidate_product_concepts.problem_it_solves: Compliance violations, complex accounting (GL, AR/AP, assessments), and meeting governance workflows for HOAs.

assumptions.inference: The platform must replicate Vantaca's 'System of Action' (Action Items) methodology.
assumptions.basis: Referenced File: Vantaca Product Description - 'Vantaca HOAi... An AI-first automation engine... everything in Vantaca is an Action Item'.

assumptions.inference: The MVP targets 'Production Grade' financial handling (Auditable, compliant GL structure).
assumptions.basis: Referenced File: Hestami CDM Domain 2 — Accounting — Requirements: 'Auditable, compliant GL structure'.

assumptions.inference: Integration with government services (Permits/BLDS) is critical for the ARC domain.
assumptions.basis: Raw Intent Appendix: Pillar 1 Vision mentions 'integration with government services for actions such as permits review and applications'.

assumptions.inference: The MVP assumes a 'Single Tenant' or 'Multi-Tenant' model where HOAs are distinct tenants.
assumptions.basis: Raw Intent Architecture: 'PostgreSQL ... Row-Level Security (RLS) to enforce strict multi-tenant isolation'.

assumptions.inference: AI extraction of documents (CC&Rs, Bylaws) is a core feature, not an afterthought.
assumptions.basis: Referenced File: Hestami CDM Domain 10 — Documents & Records: 'AI-driven extraction, classification, and summarization are core enhancement features'.

constraints: Must use Cerbos for policy-based authorization engine (Board vs. Staff access).
Must support Statutory deadlines (Notice requirements) via Rules Engine.
Must support Mass Email/SMS (Domain 7) for violation notices and meeting notices.
Must adhere to Vantaca model for CAM workflows.
Must support Reserve Studies and Funding Plans (Domain 11).
open_questions: How to handle the integration with legacy property management software?
What is the specific data retention policy for 'Eternal Perspective' historical data?
Which specific trade associations (PLUM, HVAC) are prioritized for compliance tracking?

candidate_product_concepts.name: Field Service Engine MVP (Phase 2 Focus)
candidate_product_concepts.description: Implementation focused exclusively on Pillar 2 (Service Provider Field Services Management) to prioritize dispatching, technician operations, and commercial terms for contractors.
candidate_product_concepts.problem_it_solves: Manual dispatching, lack of job costing, difficulty managing recurring services, and mobile access to work orders.

assumptions.inference: The MVP must function as an 'Operating System for the Trades' (ServiceTitan Model).
assumptions.basis: Referenced File: ServiceTitan Vision: 'Building the operating system for the trades'.

assumptions.inference: Mobile App is a critical component for offline-first capabilities.
assumptions.basis: Raw Intent Architecture: 'Mobile... Dedicated clients for... technicians, focusing on task-centric workflows and offline-first capabilities'.

assumptions.inference: Job Costing and Profitability are required immediately to attract contractors.
assumptions.basis: Referenced File: ServiceTitan Key Features: 'Project Management & Job Costing... breaking down costs across materials, equipment, commissions, and labor'.

assumptions.inference: The platform must support 'Offline mode with queueing' for remote sites.
assumptions.basis: Referenced File: ServiceTitan Domain 6 — Field Technician Mobile Operations: 'Offline mode with queueing'.

assumptions.inference: The MVP assumes a 'Good-Better-Best' quoting model is needed for estimates.
assumptions.basis: Referenced File: ServiceTitan Domain 7 — Estimates: 'Multi-tier proposal options'.

constraints: Must use DBOS for durable, versioned workflows (Domain 3).
Must integrate with Stripe/Square for Payment capture (Domain 7).
Must use Traefik as the sole ingress proxy.
Must use FFmpeg for video processing (evidence capture).
Must strictly follow the ServiceTitan business domains (Identity, Workforce, Pricebook, etc.).
open_questions: Should the MVP support 'Freelance' technicians or only 'Agency' technicians?
How to handle the integration with accounting software (QuickBooks, Sage) for contractors?
What is the SLA timer logic for 'Emergency vs Routine' prioritization?
